NextAuth.js NEXTAUTH_URL must be set in production or callbacks break
body:
NextAuth.js uses `NEXTAUTH_URL` to construct callback URLs. If it's missing in production, OAuth redirects go to `localhost` and everything breaks silently.

```bash
# .env.production
NEXTAUTH_URL=https://myapp.com
NEXTAUTH_SECRET=generate-with-openssl-rand-base64-32

# Common mistake: forgetting to set in Vercel/Railway/Fly.io environment variables
# NextAuth falls back to localhost:3000 in production!
```

On Vercel, NextAuth auto-detects `VERCEL_URL` but it uses the `*.vercel.app` domain, not your custom domain. Always set `NEXTAUTH_URL` explicitly to your production domain. Also ensure `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` is set — in development it's auto-generated, but production requires an explicit value.

title: Cookie Secure flag prevents cookies from being sent over HTTP in development
Timing attacks on string comparison leak password hash information
Passport.js authenticate must be called as middleware, not as a regular function
Session fixation attack when session ID persists after authentication
argon2 WASM build fails in serverless environments without native bindings
TOTP 2FA codes are valid for 30-second windows — check adjacent windows

JWT kid header allows key confusion attacks if not validated against allowlist
bcrypt cost factor 10 is too low for modern hardware — use 12 minimum
OAuth implicit flow is deprecated — use authorization code with PKCE instead
body:
The OAuth implicit flow (response_type=token) returns access tokens directly in the URL fragment, exposing them to browser history, referrer headers, and JavaScript.

```
# DEPRECATED — implicit flow
https://auth.example.com/authorize?
  response_type=token&         # Token in URL fragment!
  client_id=CLIENT_ID&
  redirect_uri=https://myapp.com/callback

# Redirect: https://myapp.com/callback#access_token=eyJhbG...
# Token visible in: browser history, logs, any script on the page
```

**Use authorization code + PKCE instead:**
```
# Step 1: Authorization request
https://auth.example.com/authorize?
  response_type=code&           # Code, not token
  client_id=CLIENT_ID&
  redirect_uri=https://myapp.com/callback&
  code_challenge=CHALLENGE&
  code_challenge_method=S256

# Step 2: Exchange code for token (server-to-server)
POST https://auth.example.com/token
  code=AUTH_CODE&
  code_verifier=VERIFIER&
  grant_type=authorization_code
```

The authorization code is short-lived (typically 60 seconds) and can only be exchanged once. PKCE ensures the code can only be exchanged by the client that initiated the flow. All major OAuth providers support PKCE.

title: JsonWebTokenError: jwt malformed — token is not a valid JWT string
TokenExpiredError: jwt expired — handling expired tokens gracefully
Error: secret or public key must be provided — jwt.verify missing key
CORS preflight OPTIONS request returns 401 because auth middleware runs first
Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:6379 — Redis session store connection failure
NextAuth Error: OAUTH_CALLBACK_ERROR — redirect_uri mismatch
body:
OAuth providers reject callbacks when the redirect URI in the token exchange doesn't match the one registered in the provider's developer console.

```
[next-auth][error][OAUTH_CALLBACK_ERROR]
https://next-auth.js.org/errors#oauth_callback_error
redirect_uri_mismatch
```

**Common causes:**
```
# Registered in provider: https://myapp.com/api/auth/callback/google
# Actual request from:    https://www.myapp.com/api/auth/callback/google  ← www mismatch!
# Or:                     http://myapp.com/api/auth/callback/google   ← http vs https!
# Or:                     https://myapp.com/api/auth/callback/google/ ← trailing slash!
```

**Fix checklist:**
1. Copy the exact callback URL from NextAuth: `{NEXTAUTH_URL}/api/auth/callback/{provider}`
2. Add it to your OAuth provider's console (Google Cloud Console, GitHub Settings, etc.)
3. Ensure no www vs non-www mismatch
4. Ensure no http vs https mismatch
5. Ensure no trailing slash mismatch
6. For local dev: add `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/callback/{provider}`

```typescript
// The callback URL is determined by NEXTAUTH_URL:
// NEXTAUTH_URL=https://myapp.com → /api/auth/callback/google
// Make sure this EXACT URL is in your provider's allowed redirect URIs
```

Error: Unexpected token < in JSON — OAuth token endpoint returning HTML error
Error: EPERM operation not permitted — bcrypt fails on Alpine Linux Docker
body:
bcrypt's native bindings fail to build on Alpine Linux because it uses musl libc instead of glibc.

```
npm ERR! gyp ERR! build error
Error: EPERM: operation not permitted
# Or: Error: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libm.so.6 not found
```

**Fix options:**
```dockerfile
# Option 1: Install build dependencies on Alpine
FROM node:20-alpine
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++
COPY . .
RUN npm install

# Option 2: Use bcryptjs (pure JavaScript, no native deps)
# npm install bcryptjs
import bcrypt from 'bcryptjs'; // Drop-in replacement API

# Option 3: Use non-Alpine image
FROM node:20-slim
# native modules work without extra dependencies
```

`bcryptjs` is 30% slower than native bcrypt but has zero native dependencies. For production, use the native `bcrypt` with proper build tools or switch to `argon2` with `@node-rs/argon2` (pre-built WASM binaries).
